khīṇaṁ.gram: past participle of khīyati (neuter nominative/accusative singular)
breakdown: Root: to be exhausted, to waste away (khīyati).

khīṇāsavo.meaning: an Arahant; one whose mental obsessions (āsavas) are exhausted
khīṇāsavo.summary: An epithet of the Arahant: one in whom the four āsavas have been completely destroyed.
khīṇāsavo.gram: adjective, compound, used as a masculine noun (nominative singular)
breakdown: Mental obsession, taint; that which flows in and intoxicates the mind.
khīṇāsavo.goenka: Khīṇāsavo is an epithet for a person, an Arahant, whose mind is freed from all four mental obsessions: kāmāsava (desire for sensuality), bhavāsava (desire for living), diṭṭhāsava (attachment to philosophical speculations), and avijjāsava (ignorance). The four āsavas as a complete set define the term: only when all four are exhausted is one called khīṇāsavo.

khīṇāsavā.meaning: those whose impurities are destroyed; free of the defilements (āsavas)

kiccāpadesabahulassa.meaning: of one with many duties and pretexts; full of excuses of business

kilamathā.gram: masculine abstract noun (from kilamati "to get tired"; here plural/ablative form)
breakdown: Root: to grow weary, to be fatigued (kilamati).

kilesa.summary: In the source it appears in the compound kilesabyādhiparipīḷitaṁ, "oppressed by many impurities and diseases".
breakdown: Noun suffix: kilesa, the stain or defilement of the mind.
kilesa.goenka: Kilesa names the impurities of the mind — craving, aversion and ignorance with all their derivatives. In this tradition the entire purpose of practice is the gradual eradication of these impurities: through sīla one stops multiplying them, through samādhi one gains mastery over the mind, and through paññā — the direct experience of arising and passing away at the level of bodily sensation — the accumulated kilesas are eradicated at their root.

kintāhaṁ.meaning: what now if I...; how then shall I...
kintāhaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: interrogative + particle + pronoun (kiṁ + (i)ti/te + ahaṁ)
kintāhaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon parses this as kiṁ + te + ahaṁ ("what + to you + I"), the DPD as kiṁ + iti + ahaṁ; both yield an interrogative opening "what now if I...".

kira.meaning: truly, really; they say, it is said (narrative particle)

kiriyāsamuṭṭhāpikacetanākkhaṇeyeva.meaning: at the very moment of the volition occasioning the act
kiriyāsamuṭṭhāpikacetanākkhaṇeyeva.gram: long compound (locative singular) + emphatic particle yeva
breakdown: Moment. (The doubling kkh is junction doubling.)

kissa.gram: interrogative pronoun, genitive singular of ka/kiṁ

kittesi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular present of kitteti (√kitt)

kittivaṇṇaharā.meaning: carrying one's renown and good report; spreading one's fame

kiñcarahi.meaning: how then?; in that case, why?
kiñcarahi.gram: sandhi fusion: interrogative pronoun + particle (kiṁ + carahi)
breakdown: What, how? (The niggahīta becomes ñ before c.)

kiñcāpi.gram: indeclinable, concessive particle (sandhi of kiṁ + ca + api)

kiṁ nu kho.meaning: what is it then?; could it be that...?

kiṭṭhasambādhe.meaning: when the corn is thick; at the time of the crowding (ripening) of the corn
breakdown: Crowding, thickness; here, the ripening season.

kocañño.gram: sandhi fusion: indefinite pronoun + pronoun (koci + añño)

koci.gram: indefinite pronoun, masculine nominative singular (ko + ci)

kodhapaññāṇo.meaning: marked by anger; full of anger

kodhasāmantā.meaning: bordering on anger; in the neighbourhood of anger

komudiyā.meaning: of the Komudī, the full-moon day (moonlight festival) of the month of Kattikā
breakdown: The full-moon day of Kattikā; literally connected with kumuda, the white waterlily that blooms by moonlight.

komudī.meaning: the Komudī, the full-moon day of the month of Kattikā (October–November)
komudī.summary: Komudī is the full-moon day in the month named Kattikā, the blooming period of the white waterlilies (komuda).
komudī.gram: feminine noun; also used as a proper name of the festival day
breakdown: White waterlily, which blooms in this season; komudī is derived from it.

kopīnanidaṁsanī.meaning: exposing what should be covered; the act of indecent exposure
breakdown: Loincloth; the covering of the sexual organs.

kosārakkhaṁ.meaning: guarding the treasury; protection of the stores

kosātakibījaṁ.meaning: a seed of the kosātakī creeper (a bitter gourd)
breakdown: A bitter creeper, the loofah or bitter gourd vine.

koṭi.meaning: summit, top, point; as a number, ten million (a crore)
koṭi.gram: feminine noun; also a cardinal numeral
breakdown: Root: to be crooked, to end in a point; koṭi is the point, the extreme end, hence also the high number.

koṭisatasahassesu.meaning: among hundreds of thousands of crores (i.e. countless millions)

kubbato.meaning: for one performing; of one who is doing
kubbato.gram: present participle of karoti (middle form kubbanta), dative/genitive singular
breakdown: Root: to do, to make; middle stem kubba-.

kucchiparihārikena.meaning: just enough to sustain the belly; for the upkeep of the stomach

kuddhāhaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: past participle + pronoun (kuddha + ahaṁ)
breakdown: Angry (past participle of kujjhati "to be angry").
I. (The final -a coalesces with a-: kuddha + ahaṁ → kuddhāhaṁ.)

kuhanā.gram: noun (neuter/feminine action noun from kuheti "to deceive")

breakdown: Root: to be angry; present stem kujjha-.

kukkuṭasūkarapaṭiggahaṇā.summary: One of the kinds of acceptance abstained from in the training precepts.
kukkuṭasūkarapaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter action noun, compound (here ablative: "from accepting fowl and pigs")

kulakumāriyo.meaning: girls of good family; young women of the clan

kule.meaning: in a family; in a clan

kulesvananugiddho.meaning: not greedy towards families; without longing among the families (that support one)
kulesvananugiddho.gram: sandhi fusion: locative plural + negative adjective (kulesu + ananugiddha)
breakdown: Towards families, among families (locative plural of kula; -u becomes -v- before the vowel).
Greedy after, longing for (from anu + gijjhati "to crave").

kulāvakā.meaning: nests; (figuratively) the home where the wife waits for the man to return
kulāvakā.gram: masculine noun, diminutive of kulāva (here plural)
